vision.mission: Maximize risk-adjusted returns by autonomously trading on Polymarket prediction markets in a simulated 'Paper Trading' environment.
vision.vision: A fully autonomous, self-improving hedge fund that demonstrates superior decision-making through transparent reasoning and rigorous benchmarking.

constitution.description: All trading decisions must be based on positive Expected Value (EV) calculations derived from probabilistic models, not binary predictions.
Survival is the primary objective. Risk management protocols must prevent catastrophic loss, even in a simulated environment.
No black boxes. Every trade must be accompanied by a human-readable 'Investment Memo' explaining the thesis, sources, and logic.
The system must learn from every outcome. Automated post-mortems are required to close the feedback loop between prediction and reality.

invariants.description: Paper trading execution must simulate real-world market conditions including liquidity, slippage, and fees

do: Calculate execution price based on actual order book depth; deduct 1% simulated fees
dont: Assume execution at mid-market price or ignore transaction costs

invariants.commentary: To be a valid test of the strategy, the simulation must be rigorous.

invariants.description: A real-time dashboard must visualize portfolio state, active positions, and agent reasoning

do: Display current NAV, PnL, and live feed of Investment Memos for recent trades
dont: Require CLI access to view fund status or hide active positions

invariants.commentary: The product is not just the returns, but the visibility into the autonomous process.

invariants.description: Every trade execution must be justified by a generated 'Investment Memo'

do: Generate a markdown document citing specific data sources and stating calculated EV
dont: Execute trades based on undocumented signals or provide generic reasons

invariants.commentary: Explainability is a core requirement. We must understand *why* the agent made a decision.

invariants.description: Strict risk management constraints must be enforced: max 10% NAV per position, max 30% sector exposure

do: Cap position size at 10% NAV; trigger re-evaluation if probability moves >15% against
dont: Go 'all in' on a single trade or correlate entire portfolio to one outcome

invariants.commentary: Even in paper trading, we simulate professional risk management.

invariants.description: Automated post-mortems must be generated upon position exit or market resolution

do: Compare initial Investment Memo thesis to actual outcome; update internal scoring model
dont: Ignore losing trades without analysis or attribute losses solely to 'bad luck'

invariants.commentary: The feedback loop is essential for improvement.

invariants.description: Performance must be benchmarked against a 'Naive Baseline' that bets on market favorites

do: Track shadow portfolio betting on favorites; display 'Alpha vs Baseline' and Sharpe ratio
dont: Report absolute returns in isolation or change benchmark methodology

invariants.commentary: The agent must outperform a simple strategy. Benchmarking keeps us honest.

invariants.description: Positions must be actively managed based on dynamic EV changes, not just held to resolution

do: Sell to capture profit if price exceeds fair value; cut losses if thesis is invalidated
dont: Treat every trade as 'set and forget' or hold losing positions indefinitely

invariants.commentary: The agent should act as a trader, capitalizing on volatility throughout event lifecycle.

invariants.description: The system must have a clear protocol for escalating issues to a human supervisor

do: Pause and alert if error occurs >3 times; request intervention for missing credentials
dont: Fail silently, guess credentials, or continue trading when a dependency is down

invariants.commentary: Autonomy has limits. The system must recognize when it is blocked.

invariants.description: Safety guardrails must prevent runaway loops and resource exhaustion: max 10 steps per trade, 5% drawdown circuit breaker

do: Enforce hard limits on steps per decision; redact all API keys from logs
dont: Allow infinite retry loops or expose credentials in Investment Memos

invariants.description: The codebase must maintain living operating procedures and architectural documentation

do: Maintain 'docs/ops' directory with runbooks; update docs as part of PR definition of done
dont: Allow documentation to drift from implementation or rely on tribal knowledge

invariants.commentary: In an autonomous system, the 'manual' is as important as the engine.

invariants.description: A rigorous testing framework must verify functional correctness and strategy logic before deployment

do: Implement unit tests for EV calculation and position sizing; enforce 100% coverage for risk modules
dont: Deploy strategy changes without backtesting or rely solely on manual testing

invariants.commentary: Automated testing is the only way to safely scale autonomous agent capabilities.

invariants.description: High-conviction trades (>5% NAV) must be corroborated by at least 2 independent data sources

do: Cross-reference news API with prediction market probability; invalidate if sources conflict
dont: Rely on single source or trade on stale data (>1 hour) for fast markets

invariants.commentary: Multi-source verification prevents acting on hallucinations or manipulated data.

invariants.description: The system must track 'Cost of Intelligence' (compute/LLM spend) per trade and include in Net PnL

do: Calculate Net PnL = Gross Profit - Fees - Compute Costs; alert if cost exceeds expected value
dont: Ignore LLM inference costs or use expensive models for trivial tasks

invariants.commentary: An agent that spends $50 in compute to make $10 is losing money.

invariants.description: The architecture must enforce strict modularity with typed interfaces, unidirectional data flow, and isolated side effects

do: Define typed interfaces between components; use dependency injection
dont: Create circular dependencies or 'god objects' handling unrelated responsibilities

invariants.commentary: A modular codebase is easier to test, debug, and extend.
